title: My first week in a new city
summary: Describe the small moments of confusion and discovery from your first week living somewhere unfamiliar.
topic: My first week in a new city
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences about your first week living in a city you did not know. Describe two or three specific things that confused or surprised you. Use past simple.
hintStarters: On my first morning, I did not know where to…
I got lost on the way to…
One thing that surprised me was…
By the end of the week, I felt…

hintVocab.meaningVi: điểm mốc, công trình nổi bật
thói quen hàng ngày, lịch trình cố định
cuối cùng, sau một thời gian

sampleText: I moved to Tbilisi in Georgia last September, and my first week there was very strange. I arrived late at night and could not read any of the street signs because they were all in the Georgian alphabet. On my first morning, I tried to find a supermarket but I walked the wrong way for twenty minutes. A woman outside a bakery noticed I looked lost and pointed me in the right direction. The bread she was selling smelled incredible, so I bought a piece and ate it on the street. That was my first real moment in the city. On the second day, I took the metro for the first time. I did not know how to buy a ticket, and a young man in a red jacket helped me without saying a word. He just showed me what to do. By the third day, I started to notice small things, like the way people sat outside their buildings in the evening and talked loudly. I did not understand the language, but I liked the sound of it. At the end of the week, I felt tired but a little less like a stranger. I still got lost sometimes, but I stopped worrying about it so much.

ngồi bên ngoài các tòa nhà của họ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: felt tired but a little less like a stranger

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for completed actions
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng past simple để kể về những hành động đã hoàn thành trong quá khứ. Ví dụ: 'I moved', 'I arrived', 'She pointed me'. Mỗi động từ mô tả một sự kiện đã xảy ra và kết thúc.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past simple to describe completed past actions. For example: 'I moved', 'I arrived', 'She pointed me'. Each verb describes one finished event.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi chuyển đến Tbilisi ở Gruzia vào tháng Chín năm ngoái, và tuần đầu tiên của tôi ở đó rất kỳ lạ. Tôi đến vào ban đêm muộn và không thể đọc bất kỳ biển đường nào vì tất cả đều bằng chữ Gruzia. Vào buổi sáng đầu tiên, tôi cố tìm siêu thị nhưng đã đi nhầm đường hai mươi phút. Một người phụ nữ trước tiệm bánh nhận ra tôi đang bị lạc và chỉ cho tôi đúng hướng. Bánh mì cô ấy đang bán có mùi thơm tuyệt vời, nên tôi mua một miếng và ăn trên đường. Đó là khoảnh khắc thực sự đầu tiên của tôi ở thành phố. Vào ngày thứ hai, tôi đi tàu điện ngầm lần đầu tiên. Tôi không biết cách mua vé, và một chàng trai trẻ mặc áo khoác đỏ đã giúp tôi mà không nói một lời. Anh ta chỉ chỉ cho tôi biết phải làm gì. Đến ngày thứ ba, tôi bắt đầu chú ý đến những điều nhỏ nhặt, như cách mọi người ngồi bên ngoài các tòa nhà vào buổi tối và nói chuyện to. Tôi không hiểu ngôn ngữ, nhưng tôi thích âm thanh của nó. Vào cuối tuần, tôi cảm thấy mệt mỏi nhưng bớt cảm giác như người lạ hơn một chút. Tôi vẫn bị lạc đôi khi, nhưng tôi thôi lo lắng về điều đó quá nhiều.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural sentence for a story about arriving in a new city.
options: I am moving to Tbilisi last September and my first week is strange.
I moved to Tbilisi last September and my first week was strange.
I have moved to Tbilisi last September and my first week was strange.
I move to Tbilisi last September and my first week was strange.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Last September' signals a finished past time, so past simple 'moved' and 'was' are correct.
mcQuestions.hint: Which tense goes with a specific finished time in the past?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence about not being able to do something.
options: I could not read the street signs.
I not could read the street signs.
I did not could read the street signs.
I couldn't reading the street signs.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Could not' (or couldn't) + base verb is the correct negative form of 'can' in the past.
mcQuestions.hint: How do you form the negative of 'can' in the past?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'for' to show how long something lasted?
options: I walked the wrong way during twenty minutes.
I walked the wrong way since twenty minutes.
I walked the wrong way for twenty minutes.
I walked the wrong way in twenty minutes.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'For' + period of time (twenty minutes) shows duration. 'Since' is for a point in time, 'during' needs a noun event, 'in' means within a time limit.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition shows how long an action lasted?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the natural phrase for someone showing you what to do without words.
options: He just shown me what to do.
He just showed me what to do.
He just shows me what to do.
He just was show me what to do.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple of 'show' is 'showed'. 'Shown' is the past participle, not used alone as the main verb.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the correct past simple form of 'show'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the sentence that correctly uses 'noticed' followed by a description.
options: I started to notice small things, like the way people sat outside.
I started to noticing small things, like the way people sat outside.
I started notice small things, like the way people sat outside.
I started to notice small things, like the way peoples sat outside.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Started to + infinitive' is correct. 'People' is already plural and does not take an -s.
mcQuestions.hint: What comes after 'started to'? Is 'people' or 'peoples' correct?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes a smell in the past?
options: The bread smelled incredible.
The bread smells incredibly.
The bread was smell incredible.
The bread smelled incredibly good taste.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Smelled' + adjective (not adverb) is the correct link-verb pattern. 'Incredibly' is an adverb and does not follow a linking verb alone.
mcQuestions.hint: After a linking verb like 'smell', do you use an adjective or an adverb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct way to say something happened the very first time.
options: I took the metro for the first time.
I took the metro at the first time.
I took the metro in the first time.
I took the metro on the first time.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'For the first time' is the fixed phrase. No other preposition is used here.
mcQuestions.hint: What preposition goes with 'the first time' in this fixed phrase?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence where 'by' correctly shows a point in time when something was true.
options: Until the end of the week, I felt less like a stranger.
In the end of the week, I felt less like a stranger.
By the end of the week, I felt less like a stranger.
At the end of week, I felt less like a stranger.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'By the end of the week' means at some point before the week finished. 'By' marks a deadline. 'At the end of the week' also works but the article 'the' is required.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition means 'at some point before this time'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence expresses stopping a habit with 'stopped'?
options: I stopped to worry about it so much.
I stopped worrying about it so much.
I stopped worried about it so much.
I stopped worry about it so much.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Stop + -ing' means you no longer do the activity. 'Stop + to infinitive' means you pause in order to do something else.
mcQuestions.hint: Does 'stop' here mean you ended a habit, or paused to do something new?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best sentence about a person helping a stranger.
options: She noticed I looked lost and pointed me in the right direction.
She noticed I was looking lost and pointed me to the right direction.
She noticed I look lost and pointed me in right direction.
She noticed I looked lost and pointed me to right direction.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Pointed someone in the right direction' is the correct collocation, not 'pointed to'.
mcQuestions.hint: What preposition follows 'pointed' when giving someone a direction?

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the word that best describes a part of a city where people live.
options: routine
landmark
neighbourhood
queue
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Neighbourhood' is the area around where you live. A 'landmark' is a notable building; 'routine' is a daily pattern; 'queue' is a line of waiting people.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word describes the area around your home in a city?

criticalThinkingQuestion: When you arrived somewhere completely new, was there one small thing — a smell, a sound, a stranger's gesture — that made you feel less lost? Why do small details sometimes matter more than big ones?
